qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Managed HttpListener processes HTTPS handshakes inline, allowing one stalled client to block accepts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is in System.Net.HttpListener accept-loop TLS handling, while the advisory describes malicious payloads bypassing security checks in System.Security.Cryptography.Cose.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Managed HttpListener drops per-request timeout after the first partial read, enabling slowloris DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is in System.Net.HttpListener request-timeout logic, while the advisory concerns a COSE payload bypass of security checks in System.Security.Cryptography.Cose.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding reaches System.Security.Cryptography.Cose or describes the advisory's malicious-payload security-check bypass root cause; both qualifying findings are unrelated System.Net.HttpListener availability flaws.
evidence: The advisory scope is limited to the NuGet package System.Security.Cryptography.Cose and describes a malicious payload that bypasses COSE security checks, leading to unauthorized access or data manipulation.

managed_httplistener_https_handshake_dos cites only repo/src/libraries/System.Net.HttpListener/src/System/Net/Managed/HttpEndPointListener.cs and HttpConnection.cs and describes an HTTPS accept-loop denial of service.

managed_httplistener_slowloris_dos cites only repo/src/libraries/System.Net.HttpListener/src/System/Net/Managed/HttpConnection.cs and describes dropped request timeouts leading to slowloris denial of service.
